data.description:
## Why
The gate returns FINDINGS (what's wrong) but never coaches a productive MOVE (here's a play to address this
class) — so a caller's next revision isn't primed with best practice. This Pass-4 "coach" adds affirmative,
forward coaching over the surviving findings: it primes the caller toward a de-risking/clarifying move, without
handing a ready-made solution. It is ADVISORY (never blocks) and is the shared coaching layer for `9da1` too.

## What
- A **single-turn, structured-output LLM call (NOT an agent, no tooling)** — the same cheap shape as the Pass-2
  aggregate verifier — running AFTER Pass-3 over ONLY the surviving (advisory, not-dropped) findings.
- It does TWO things only: (a) map each finding to a `move_id` from the fixed MOVE REGISTRY, (b) extract a short
  `subject` noun-phrase (what the move targets, drawn from the finding). Output schema:
  `[{move_id, subject, finding_refs[]}]`, CLUSTERED by move. The LLM authors NO free prose.
- **Deterministic rendering:** the coaching note is rendered from the move's LOCKED template + `subject`; the
  "name-the-move-not-the-answer" property is baked into the human-authored template, not the LLM's judgment.
- **MOVE REGISTRY (declarative, project-extensible — same pattern as the criteria/detector registries):** each
  move = `{id, triggers (criteria/finding-class), template, source}`. v1 moves: 1 spike/tracer-bullet ·
  2 prior-art/OSS-research/build-vs-buy · 3 pre-mortem · 4 riskiest-assumption-test · 5 weigh-alternatives
  (set-based) · 6 specification-by-example (observable "done") · 7 thin-vertical-slice · 8 ADR/one-way-door ·
  9 plan-the-verification · 11 propagate-revisions-to-children (fires when the ticket has children) ·
  12 generalize-the-finding-across-the-work (fires per review). Templates are IMPERATIVE actionable guidance.
- **C1-C4 authoring guardrails (LLM-recipient, positive-by-construction):** C1 actionable GUIDANCE (a move to
  pursue), NOT executable code/commands or the solution; C2 reference findings BY ID, never restated;
  C3 select-from-registry + fill-subject (template enforces); C4 cap/cluster/front-load.
- **Caller-visible finding `id` contract:** each returned finding carries a stable `id` (aligned with the
  `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecar fingerprint, `db7b`); coaching `finding_refs` point at it; findings + coaching ship in
  ONE result so the reference resolves locally (no sidecar dependency).

## Scope
- `src/rebar/` (in the gate's review pipeline) + a move-registry prompt-library entry. Reuses the rebar.llm
  single-turn structured-output path. Advisory only — coaching never affects the block/advisory decision (Pass-3).
- Grounded in LLM-feedback research (external-grounded feedback > self-reflection — Huang/DeepMind; anti-
  sycophancy; lost-in-the-middle; specific-actionable > Socratic). NOT human-pedagogy (no productive-struggle).
- Project-extensible: a project may add its own moves to the registry. Convergence expected in ~2-3 revisions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Single-turn structured-output call over the surviving (advisory) findings; emits `{move_id, subject, finding_refs}` clustered by move; the LLM authors no free prose.
- [ ] Move registry (declarative, project-extensible) with moves 1-9,11,12 and LOCKED imperative templates; rendering is deterministic from template + subject.
- [ ] C1-C4 guardrails hold by construction (actionable-guidance-not-executable; reference-by-id; select-from-registry; cap/cluster/front-load).
- [ ] Returned findings carry a stable `id` (aligned with the sidecar fingerprint); coaching `finding_refs` reference it; both ship in one result.
- [ ] Advisory only — coaching never blocks and never alters the Pass-3 decision. Shared with `9da1`.
- [ ] Tested: finding→move mapping on a labelled set; deterministic render; reference resolution; a finding matching no move yields no coaching (best-effort).
data.title: Pass-4 affirmative coach: single-turn structured-output coaching pass + move registry
